scenarios.description: M28 AC-8: R4 rule — POST to financial-context route produces confidential without any PII category evidence
M28 AC-8: R1 rule beats R3 — preserve=true capability with PII produces restricted, not confidential
M28 AC-8: zero-evidence guard — capability with no method, path, PII, auth, or preserve produces unknown
M29 AC-8: R1 rule — middleware matching bearer/jwt pattern produces auth_scheme=bearer with evidence_source=middleware_name
M29 AC-4: zero-evidence guard — capability with no middleware, no path, no sensitivity signal produces auth_scheme=unknown (NOT none) with empty arrays
M29 AC-3: scope/role extraction with order-preserving case-sensitive dedup from middleware annotations
M29 R2: middleware matching api_key pattern produces auth_scheme=api_key with evidence_source=middleware_name
M29 R3: middleware matching session pattern produces auth_scheme=session with evidence_source=middleware_name
M29 R4: middleware matching basic pattern produces auth_scheme=basic with evidence_source=middleware_name
M29 R5: middleware matching oauth pattern produces auth_scheme=oauth with evidence_source=middleware_name
M30: tusq surface plan --json produces byte-identical stdout across three runs on the same manifest; every gated capability reason is one of the six closed gated_reason enum values

scenarios.description: M31: tusq domain index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; aggregation_key enum is closed; unknown bucket appears last; manifest first-appearance ordering is preserved

M32: tusq effect index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; side_effect_class bucket-key enum is closed (four values); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (read → write → destructive → unknown)

M33: tusq sensitivity index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; sensitivity_class bucket-key enum is closed (five values); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (public → internal → confidential → restricted → unknown)

M34: tusq method index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; http_method bucket-key enum is closed (six values: GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: method/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (GET → POST → PUT → PATCH → DELETE, then unknown last)

M35: tusq auth index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; auth_scheme bucket-key enum is closed (seven values: bearer/api_key/session/basic/oauth/none/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: scheme/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (bearer → api_key → session → basic → oauth → none, then unknown last)

M36: tusq confidence index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; confidence_tier bucket-key enum is closed (four values: high/medium/low/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (high → medium → low, then unknown last); null/missing confidence → unknown (no warning); out-of-range confidence → unknown with warning entry in warnings[]

M37: tusq pii index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; pii_field_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (five values: none/low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (none → low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing/non-array pii_fields → unknown (with warning); non-string element in pii_fields → unknown with warning

M38: tusq examples index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; examples_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (five values: none/low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (none → low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing/not-array examples → unknown (with warning); null or array element in examples → unknown with warning

M39: tusq input index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; required_input_field_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (five values: none/low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (none → low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing/non-object input_schema → unknown (with warning); non-array or malformed required → unknown with warning; required[] containing non-string or empty-string element → unknown with warning

M40: tusq output index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; output_schema_property_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (five values: none/low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (none → low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing/non-object output_schema → unknown (with warning); null/non-object output_schema.properties → unknown with warning; properties containing non-object descriptor → unknown with warning; type:array schemas (no properties key) → unknown with reason output_schema_properties_field_missing (informative, not a defect)

M41: tusq path index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; path_segment_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (five values: none/low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (none → low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing path → unknown (with warning); non-string path → unknown with warning; empty-string path → unknown with warning; path without leading '/' → unknown with warning; path with empty interior segment (double slash or trailing slash other than '/') → unknown with warning; path='/' → none (0 segments); path parameters (:id, :orgId) count as one segment each — not unwrapped

M42: tusq response index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; output_schema_top_level_type bucket-key enum is closed (seven values: object/array/string/number/boolean/null/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: type/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (object → array → string → number → boolean → null, then unknown last); null/missing/non-object output_schema → unknown (with warning); non-object/null output_schema → unknown with warning; missing/non-string output_schema.type → unknown with warning; type string not in closed six-value set → unknown with warning; 'integer' → unknown (not coerced to number); array-of-types (type: ['object', 'null']) → unknown (type is array not string); compositional schemas without top-level type → unknown with output_schema_type_field_missing reason

M43: tusq request index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; input_schema_primary_parameter_source bucket-key enum is closed (seven values: path/request_body/query/header/mixed/none/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: source/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (path → request_body → query → header → mixed → none, then unknown last); null/missing/non-object input_schema → unknown (with warning); non-object/null input_schema → unknown with warning; missing/non-object input_schema.properties → unknown with warning; any property source outside closed four-value set → unknown with warning; cookie/file/multipart/form-data source values → unknown (not in closed set); array-of-sources → unknown (source is array not string); mixed bucket = two or more distinct closed-set source values; none bucket = properties has 0 keys

M44: tusq description index --json produces byte-identical output across three runs on the same manifest; description_word_count_tier bucket-key enum is closed (four values: low/medium/high/unknown); aggregation_key enum is closed (two values: tier/unknown); bucket iteration order is the frozen closed-enum order (low → medium → high, then unknown last); null/missing description → unknown (with warning); non-string description → unknown (with warning); empty-after-trim description → unknown (with warning); tier function = description.trim().split(/\s+/u).length; low <= 7; medium 8-14; high >= 15

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq parameter index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with none, low, high, and unknown buckets (medium deliberately absent — empty buckets must not appear). Also validates closed five-value bucket-key enum (none/low/medium/high/unknown), closed two-value aggregation_key enum (tier/unknown), bucket order determinism (none → low → high → unknown ascending-numeric-tier convention), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_property_count_tier non-persistence. Boundary thresholds 0/2/5/6 match M40 verbatim and are immutable.

Object.keys insertion-order is preserved (JSON.parse preserves key order); MUST NOT sort or reorder property keys.

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq obligation index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with required, optional, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (required/optional/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (required_status/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (required → optional → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_required_status non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved (JSON.parse preserves key order); MUST NOT sort or reorder property keys. The determinism fixture uses {c, a, b} key order with required=['a'] to verify firstKey selected is 'c' (optional, not sorted 'a' which would be required).

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq preset index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with defaulted, undefaulted, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (defaulted/undefaulted/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (default_value/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (defaulted → undefaulted → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_default_value non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved: keys={z,a,b} where z has default='seed' (defaulted), a has no default (undefaulted), b has default=0 (also defaulted per FALSY-DEFAULT-COUNTS-AS-DEFAULTED) — firstKey=z → defaulted (NOT sorted 'a' which would be undefaulted). Fifth frozen warning reason code (input_schema_properties_first_property_descriptor_invalid) formally elevated from M52/M53/M54 OBJ-004/OBJ-005/OBJ-006 pattern.

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq sample index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with exampled, unexampled, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (exampled/unexampled/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (example_set/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (exampled → unexampled → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_examples non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved: keys={z,a,b} where z has examples=["seed"] (exampled), a has no examples (unexampled), b has examples=[] (unknown with warning input_schema_properties_first_property_examples_invalid_when_present) — firstKey=z → exampled (insertion-order, NOT sorted). EMPTY-ARRAY examples:[] is unknown WITH warning (alignment with M54 empty-enum-is-malformed precedent; contrast with M55 where default:[] is defaulted per FALSY-DEFAULT-COUNTS-AS-DEFAULTED). null-as-absent: examples:null → unexampled (no warning, mirroring M55 null-as-absent). Sixth frozen warning reason code: input_schema_properties_first_property_examples_invalid_when_present (single consolidated code covers both non-array and empty-array malformations).

synthetic_capabilities.name: insertion_order_cap
synthetic_capabilities.description: Keys={z,a,b}: z has examples=["seed"] (exampled), a has no examples (unexampled), b has examples=[] (unknown+warning). firstKey=z → exampled (insertion-order, NOT sorted).

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq caption index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with titled, untitled, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (titled/untitled/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (title_label/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (titled → untitled → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_title non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved: keys={z,a,b} where z has title='Subscription ID' (titled), a has no title (untitled), b has title='' (unknown with warning input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present) — firstKey=z → titled (insertion-order, NOT sorted). EMPTY-STRING title:'' is unknown WITH warning (alignment with M54 empty-enum-is-malformed and M56 empty-array-is-malformed precedents). null-as-absent: title:null → untitled (no warning, mirroring M55/M56 null-as-absent). WHITESPACE-ONLY title:'   ' → titled (string length >= 1 is the only check; whitespace-trim deferred to M-Caption-Title-Whitespace-Distribution-Index-1). Sixth frozen warning reason code: input_schema_properties_first_property_title_invalid_when_present (single consolidated code covers both non-string and empty-string malformations).

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq legacy index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with deprecated, active, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (deprecated/active/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (lifecycle_stage/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (deprecated → active → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_deprecated non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved: keys={z,a,b} where z has deprecated=true (deprecated), a has no deprecated field (active), b has deprecated='true' (unknown with warning input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present) — firstKey=z → deprecated (insertion-order, NOT sorted). NULL-AS-ABSENT: deprecated:null → active (no warning, mirroring M55/M56/M57 null-as-absent). EXPLICIT-FALSE-IS-ACTIVE: deprecated:false → active (no warning). NO truthy/falsy coercion: deprecated:1 → unknown WITH warning. Sixth frozen warning reason code: input_schema_properties_first_property_deprecated_invalid_when_present (single consolidated code covers all non-boolean malformations: string/number/array/object).

scenarios.description: Verifies that tusq regex index produces byte-identical output across three runs on a synthetic manifest with patterned, unpatterned, not_applicable, and unknown buckets. Also validates closed four-value bucket-key enum (patterned/unpatterned/not_applicable/unknown), closed three-value aggregation_key enum (pattern_constraint/not_applicable/unknown), bucket order determinism (patterned → unpatterned → not_applicable → unknown), warnings[] always present, manifest non-mutation, and input_schema_first_property_pattern non-persistence. Object.keys insertion-order is preserved: keys={z,a,b} where z has pattern='^foo$' (patterned), a has no pattern (unpatterned), b has pattern='' (unknown with warning input_schema_properties_first_property_pattern_invalid_when_present) — firstKey=z → patterned (insertion-order, NOT sorted).
